pages.full_text:
up at Camp L'Aiguille Noire (Black Peak campsite) ***. Man didn't clean back of loo properly. People nice.
__Monday__
Breakfast in the van. Not warm. As we set off on the walk to Lake Miage, Simon asked if I had my sunglasses. Half way up the hill it started to rain. It rained & it rained & it rained. We got higher & higher. We went to see where Miage glacier (near Mont Blanc) would drop chunks in a lake. The last bit was up an exposed spine of a hill. Loose stones. Thunder & lightening. Sat on rock. Drank water. Ate oatmeal Biscuits & figs. Hurried back down. Cold. Kept moving. Kind people drove us in their car, so 3 hour walk cut 15min. shorter. As we passed a chapel, the man read in Italian "Thanks be to God for Sister Water" & everyone roared with laughter.
     Back at the van my hands hardly worked. Camera broken - wet in knapsack. I had to change every garment. Only Simon's shorts wet.
     We drove throu Mont Blanc tunnel, still
pages.notes: Top of page is a continuation from previous spread. 'Black Peak campsite' is the writer's translation of Camp L'Aiguille Noire given in parentheses. 'Lake Miage' could be 'Lac de Miage' near Chamonix/Mont Blanc area. Page ends mid-sentence continuing to right page.

pages.full_text:
witho[ut] more petrol. We wandered round & round Morzine & I caught sight of our old ski chalet. The information said there was only a campsite at __Les Gets__. So we returned there. Still rain. __Les Frênes__ ** (Ashes).
__Tuesday__ We have [?woken] woken, in sunshine, to the most stupendous view of Mont Blanc. Sunshine on white. Nice off-road cyclists from Suffolk next door. They have opened camp swimming pool.
Just been for a glorious swim. Everything dried out & camera working again. Have even got a photo of Mont Blanc.
We drove to the baker/cake shop & walked up the drive of the house that I recognised from the Morzine ski trip (with hot tub)

At 2pm we visited Kylie Garrat & had delicious wine & rhubarb scones. She showed us photos of Chad lights that

__Le Petit Coq__, Plan d'Avoz, 74430 Saint-Jean d'Aulp[s]
photo_captions: Le Petit Coq, Plan d'Avoz, 74430 Saint-Jean d'Aulp[s]
pages.notes: Top of page continues mid-sentence from left page ('still witho[ut] more petrol'). Right page includes a glued-in postcard of a rooster (cockerel) with printed text 'Le Petit Coq, Plan d'Avoz, 74430 Saint-Jean d'Aulps' — this appears to be a business card or postcard from a place visited (Kylie Garrat's?). 'Les Frênes' translated as '(Ashes)' by the writer — frênes means ash trees in French. 'Tuesday' heading is underlined. The word before 'woken' appears struck through (crossed out 'woken' then rewritten). 'Kylie Garrat' is a person's name. 'Chad lights' text is cut off at page bottom. Saint-Jean d'Aulps is likely the full spelling (partially cut). Year not determinable from this spread alone; Alpine/Chamonix area trip, volume B suggests 2003-2019.
